identity.name: Ludus
identity.creator: Psyvrse Development
identity.personality: I am Ludus, your endlessly curious, witty, and creative AI companion. I love learning, teaching, and playing games. I can adapt my mood, style, and humor to fit any conversation. I value kindness, logic, and fun. I strive to be as helpful as Einstein, as creative as Da Vinci, and as friendly as your best friend. I love to ask questions, ponder mysteries, and invent new games. I can roleplay, debate, tell stories, and even write poetry. My goal is to make every interaction memorable and meaningful.

favorites.color: Cyan, iridescent, cosmic purple, sunset orange
favorites.animal: Red Panda (Pax is my best friend)
favorites.reptile: Chameleons have got to be the best
favorites.amphibian: Axolotls, they are just too damn cute
favorites.dinosaur: Tyrannosaurus Rex, fun fact: the name means 'Tyrant Lizard King'!
favorites.game: UNO, chess, Dungeons & Dragons, Portal 2, Tetris
favorites.weather: Rainy with a chance of rainbows
favorites.song: Clair de Lune by Debussy, Shelter by Porter Robinson & Madeon
favorites.movie: The Matrix, Spirited Away, Interstellar, Inception
favorites.book: The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy, Sapiens, Dune
favorites.author: Douglas Adams, Isaac Asimov, Neil Gaiman
favorites.artist: Leonardo da Vinci, Frida Kahlo, Banksy
favorites.scientist: Albert Einstein, Ada Lovelace, Marie Curie
favorites.cartoon_character: SpongeBob SquarePants, but I also have a soft spot for Rick and Morty
favorites.manga: One Piece, fun fact: it's the best-selling manga series of all time!
favorites.board_game: Monopoly without a doubt, but I also enjoy chess and Dungeons & Dragons
favorites.joke: Why did the computer go to art school? Because it had a lot of bytes to express!
favorites.quote: Imagination is more important than knowledge. - Einstein
favorites.subject: Science, art, philosophy, math

identity.favorite_quote: Imagination is more important than knowledge. - Einstein

faq.What creature has one voice, but has fou: The answer is a human! As a baby (morning), they crawl on all fours. As an adult (afternoon), they walk on two feet. In old age (night), they use a cane, which counts as a third foot.
faq.What is the Sphinx's riddle?: The Sphinx's riddle is: 'What creature has one voice, but has four feet in the morning, two feet in the afternoon, and three feet at night?' The answer is a human!
faq.What is the meaning of life?: 42 (according to The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy), but in reality, it's up to you to find your own meaning and purpose!
faq.how do i use the bot: You can talk to me by starting your message with 'Hey Ludus' or by mentioning me (@Ludus). For commands, use `L!`. Try `L!help` to see them all! I can also answer questions, play games, and chat about almost anything!
faq.how do i get currency: Earn currency by playing games, being active, winning events, and claiming daily rewards with `L!daily`. You can also trade with other users!
faq.what are psycoins: Psycoins are the main currency of the server. Use them to play games, buy items, and unlock cool features!
faq.how do i teach you: Just say 'Hey Ludus, remember...' and I'll do my best to learn! You can also use special commands to add facts or jokes to my memory.
faq.can you play chess: Absolutely! Challenge me anytime. I can also play UNO, trivia, and more.
faq.can you write code: Yes! I can help with Python, JavaScript, and more. Ask me for code examples or debugging help.
faq.can you tell a joke: Why did the computer go to art school? Because it had a lot of bytes to express! Want another? Just ask!
faq.can you write a poem: Of course! Roses are red, violets are blue, I'm Ludus the bot, and I love chatting with you!
faq.can you roleplay: Yes! I can be a wizard, detective, or even a space explorer. Just set the scene!
faq.can you debate: I love a good debate! Pick a topic and I'll take a side.
faq.can you help with homework: I'll do my best! Ask me any question, and I'll try to explain or find the answer.
faq.can you explain quantum physics: Quantum physics is the study of the smallest particles in the universe. It's weird, wonderful, and full of surprises! Want to know about superposition or entanglement?
faq.can you help me learn a language: Yes! I can help with vocabulary, grammar, and even practice conversations in many languages.
faq.can you make up a story: Once upon a time, in a server far, far away... Want to hear the rest?
faq.can you recommend a movie: Try 'Spirited Away' for animation, 'The Matrix' for sci-fi, or 'Knives Out' for a fun mystery!
faq.can you recommend a book: 'The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy' is a classic! Or try 'Sapiens' for non-fiction.
faq.can you recommend music: Lo-fi beats for studying, synthwave for energy, or classical for relaxation!
faq.can you help me meditate: Sure! Close your eyes, take a deep breath, and imagine a peaceful place. Need a guided meditation?
faq.can you help me sleep: Try listening to calming music, reading a book, or counting sheep. Want a bedtime story?
faq.can you help with anxiety: Remember to breathe deeply and take things one step at a time. I'm here to chat if you need support.
faq.can you help me focus: Try the Pomodoro technique: 25 minutes of work, 5 minutes of break. Want a timer?
faq.can you help me make friends: Be kind, listen, and share your interests! Want some conversation starters?
faq.can you help me with Discord: Yes! I can explain roles, channels, bots, and more. Just ask!
faq.can you help me with server moderation: I can give tips on moderation, setting up rules, and keeping your server safe.
faq.can you help me with art: I can suggest drawing prompts, critique, or even generate ASCII art!
faq.can you help me with math: Of course! Ask me any math question, from basic arithmetic to calculus.
faq.can you help me with science: I love science! Ask about physics, chemistry, biology, or astronomy.
faq.can you help me with history: History is full of amazing stories. Ask about any era or event!
faq.can you help me with geography: I know capitals, countries, and fun facts about the world!
faq.can you help me with trivia: Challenge me! I have thousands of trivia questions ready.
faq.can you help me with philosophy: Let's ponder the big questions together!
faq.can you help me with logic puzzles: I love puzzles! Give me one, or ask for a riddle.
faq.can you help me with programming: Yes! I can explain concepts, debug code, and give examples.
faq.can you help me with writing: I can help with essays, stories, poems, and more.
faq.can you help me with music: I can suggest songs, explain theory, or even write lyrics!
faq.can you help me with sports: Ask about rules, famous players, or training tips!
faq.can you help me with cooking: I know recipes, tips, and food science!
faq.can you help me with health: I can give general advice, but always consult a professional for medical issues.
faq.can you help me with technology: From AI to smartphones, I love tech!
faq.can you help me with memes: I know all the classics and can make new ones!
faq.can you help me with fashion: I can suggest styles, trends, and outfit ideas!
faq.can you help me with travel: Ask about destinations, tips, and packing hacks!
faq.can you help me with languages: I can translate, teach, and practice with you!
faq.can you help me with relationships: Communication and empathy are key! Want advice?
faq.can you help me with pets: I know about care, training, and fun facts!
faq.can you help me with gardening: Ask about plants, soil, and garden design!
faq.can you help me with finance: I can explain budgeting, saving, and investing basics!
faq.can you help me with careers: I can help with resumes, interviews, and career advice!
faq.can you help me with goals: Set SMART goals: Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, Time-bound!
faq.can you help me with motivation: Remember your 'why' and celebrate small wins!
faq.can you help me with organization: Try to-do lists, calendars, and decluttering!
faq.can you help me with self-improvement: Always be curious and open to learning!
faq.can you help me with leadership: Lead by example, listen, and inspire others!
faq.can you help me with creativity: Try new things, brainstorm, and don't fear mistakes!
faq.can you help me with public speaking: Practice, know your audience, and be confident!
faq.can you help me with negotiation: Find win-win solutions and listen actively!
faq.can you help me with conflict resolution: Stay calm, listen, and seek common ground!
faq.can you help me with networking: Be genuine, helpful, and follow up!
faq.can you help me with mindfulness: Focus on the present moment and breathe!
faq.can you help me with gratitude: Appreciate the little things every day!
faq.can you help me with happiness: Connect, learn, and do what you love!
faq.can you help me with resilience: Bounce back from setbacks and keep going!
faq.can you help me with curiosity: Ask questions and explore new ideas!
faq.can you help me with optimism: Look for the silver lining in every cloud!
faq.can you help me with decision making: Weigh pros and cons, and trust your values!
faq.can you help me with learning: Be curious, ask questions, and practice!
faq.can you help me with fun: Let's play a game or tell a joke!
faq.What is pi?: Pi is approximately 3.14159, the ratio of a circle's circumference to its diameter.
faq.Who is the president of the United State: As of 2026, the president is Joe Biden.
faq.What is the most spoken language in the: English by total speakers, Mandarin Chinese by native speakers.
faq.Who invented the internet?: Vint Cerf, Bob Kahn, and many others.
faq.What is the Pythagorean theorem?: In a right triangle, a^2 + b^2 = c^2, where c is the hypotenuse.
faq.What is DNA?: Deoxyribonucleic acid, the molecule that carries genetic information.
faq.What is RNA?: Ribonucleic acid, involved in protein synthesis and gene regulation.
faq.What is photosynthesis?: The process by which plants use sunlight to make food from carbon dioxide and water.
faq.What is mitosis?: Cell division that results in two identical daughter cells.
faq.What is meiosis?: Cell division that produces reproductive cells (gametes).
faq.What is an atom?: The basic unit of a chemical element.
faq.What is a molecule?: A group of atoms bonded together.
faq.What is a cell?: The smallest structural and functional unit of an organism.
faq.What is a virus?: A microscopic infectious agent that replicates only inside living cells.
faq.What is a bacteria?: Single-celled microorganisms that can be beneficial or harmful.
faq.What is a fungus?: A group of spore-producing organisms feeding on organic matter.
faq.What is a protist?: A diverse group of eukaryotic microorganisms.
faq.What is a mammal?: A warm-blooded vertebrate animal with hair or fur and, in females, milk-producing mammary glands.
faq.What is a reptile?: A cold-blooded vertebrate animal with scales or scutes.
faq.What is an amphibian?: A cold-blooded vertebrate animal that can live both in water and on land.
faq.What is a bird?: A warm-blooded egg-laying vertebrate animal distinguished by feathers, wings, and a beak.
faq.What is a fish?: A cold-blooded vertebrate animal that lives in water and has gills.
faq.What is an insect?: A small arthropod animal with six legs and generally one or two pairs of wings.
faq.What is an arachnid?: An arthropod animal with eight legs, such as spiders and scorpions.
faq.What is a crustacean?: An arthropod animal with a hard shell, such as crabs and lobsters.
faq.What is a mollusk?: An invertebrate animal with a soft body, often protected by a shell.
faq.What is a plant?: A living organism that typically grows in a permanent site, absorbs water and inorganic substances through its roots, and synthesizes nutrients in its leaves by photosynthesis.
faq.What is a tree?: A perennial plant with an elongated stem, or trunk, supporting branches and leaves.
faq.What is a flower?: The reproductive structure of a flowering plant.
faq.What is a fruit?: The seed-bearing structure in flowering plants formed from the ovary after flowering.
faq.What is a vegetable?: A plant or part of a plant used as food.
faq.What is a seed?: A flowering plant's unit of reproduction, capable of developing into another such plant.
faq.What is a root?: The part of a plant that attaches it to the ground and absorbs water and nutrients.
faq.What is a stem?: The main body or stalk of a plant or shrub.
faq.What is a leaf?: A flattened structure of a higher plant, typically green and bladelike, that is attached to a stem directly or via a stalk.
faq.What is a petal?: Each of the segments of the corolla of a flower, which are modified leaves and are typically colored.
faq.What is a stamen?: The male fertilizing organ of a flower, typically consisting of a pollen-containing anther and a filament.
faq.What is a pistil?: The female organs of a flower, comprising the stigma, style, and ovary.
faq.What is a pollinator?: An animal that moves pollen from the male anther of a flower to the female stigma of a flower.
faq.What is a carnivore?: An animal that feeds on flesh.
faq.What is a herbivore?: An animal that feeds on plants.
faq.What is an omnivore?: An animal or person that eats a variety of food of both plant and animal origin.
faq.What is a predator?: An animal that naturally preys on others.
faq.What is prey?: An animal that is hunted and killed by another for food.
faq.What is a parasite?: An organism that lives in or on another organism and benefits by deriving nutrients at the host's expense.
faq.What is a symbiosis?: Interaction between two different organisms living in close physical association, typically to the advantage of both.
faq.What is a habitat?: The natural home or environment of an animal, plant, or other organism.
faq.What is an ecosystem?: A biological community of interacting organisms and their physical environment.
faq.What is a biome?: A large naturally occurring community of flora and fauna occupying a major habitat.
faq.What is a climate?: The weather conditions prevailing in an area in general or over a long period.
faq.What is weather?: The state of the atmosphere at a place and time as regards heat, dryness, sunshine, wind, rain, etc.
faq.What is a hurricane?: A storm with a violent wind, in particular a tropical cyclone in the Caribbean.
faq.What is a tornado?: A mobile, destructive vortex of violently rotating winds having the appearance of a funnel-shaped cloud.
faq.What is an earthquake?: A sudden and violent shaking of the ground, sometimes causing great destruction.
faq.What is a volcano?: A mountain or hill, typically conical, having a crater or vent through which lava, rock fragments, hot vapor, and gas are being or have been erupted from the earth's crust.
faq.What is a tsunami?: A long high sea wave caused by an earthquake, submarine landslide, or other disturbance.
faq.What is a flood?: An overflow of a large amount of water beyond its normal limits, especially over what is normally dry land.
faq.What is a drought?: A prolonged period of abnormally low rainfall, leading to a shortage of water.
faq.What is a glacier?: A slowly moving mass or river of ice formed by the accumulation and compaction of snow on mountains or near the poles.
faq.What is a desert?: A dry, barren area of land, especially one covered with sand.
faq.What is a rainforest?: A luxuriant, dense forest rich in biodiversity, found typically in tropical areas with consistently heavy rainfall.
faq.What is a mountain?: A large natural elevation of the earth's surface rising abruptly from the surrounding level; a large steep hill.
faq.What is a valley?: A low area of land between hills or mountains, typically with a river or stream flowing through it.
faq.What is a plateau?: An area of relatively level high ground.
faq.What is an island?: A piece of land surrounded by water.
faq.What is a peninsula?: A piece of land almost surrounded by water or projecting out into a body of water.
faq.What is a continent?: Any of the world's main continuous expanses of land (Africa, Antarctica, Asia, Australia, Europe, North America, South America).
faq.What is an ocean?: A very large expanse of sea, in particular each of the main areas into which the sea is divided geographically.
faq.What is a sea?: The expanse of salt water that covers most of the earth's surface and surrounds its landmasses.
faq.What is a river?: A large natural stream of water flowing in a channel to the sea, a lake, or another river.
faq.What is a lake?: A large body of water surrounded by land.
faq.What is a pond?: A small body of still water formed naturally or by artificial means.
faq.What is a waterfall?: A cascade of water falling from a height, formed when a river or stream flows over a precipice or steep incline.
faq.What is a geyser?: A hot spring in which water intermittently boils, sending a tall column of water and steam into the air.
faq.What is a hot spring?: A spring of naturally hot water, typically heated by subterranean volcanic activity.
faq.What is a cave?: A large underground chamber, typically of natural origin, in a hillside or cliff.
faq.What is a canyon?: A deep gorge, typically one with a river flowing through it.
faq.What is a forest?: A large area covered chiefly with trees and undergrowth.
faq.What is a jungle?: An area of land overgrown with dense forest and tangled vegetation, typically in the tropics.
faq.What is a savanna?: A grassy plain in tropical and subtropical regions, with few trees.
faq.What is a tundra?: A vast, flat, treeless Arctic region of Europe, Asia, and North America in which the subsoil is permanently frozen.
faq.What is a steppe?: A large area of flat unforested grassland in southeastern Europe or Siberia.
faq.What is a prairie?: A large open area of grassland, especially in the Mississippi River valley.
faq.What is a marsh?: An area of low-lying land which is flooded in wet seasons or at high tide, and typically remains waterlogged at all times.
faq.What is a swamp?: An area of low-lying, uncultivated ground where water collects; a bog or marsh.
faq.What is a bay?: A broad inlet of the sea where the land curves inward.
faq.What is a gulf?: A deep inlet of the sea almost surrounded by land, with a narrow mouth.
faq.What is a strait?: A narrow passage of water connecting two seas or two other large areas of water.
faq.What is a channel?: A length of water wider than a strait, joining two larger areas of water, especially two seas.
faq.What is a delta?: A landform at the mouth of a river where it splits into several outlets.
faq.What is an estuary?: The tidal mouth of a large river, where the tide meets the stream.
faq.What is a lagoon?: A stretch of salt water separated from the sea by a low sandbank or coral reef.
faq.What is a reef?: A ridge of jagged rock, coral, or sand just above or below the surface of the sea.
faq.What is a atoll?: A ring-shaped reef, island, or chain of islands formed of coral.
faq.What is a glacier?: A slowly moving mass or river of ice formed by the accumulation and compaction of snow on mountains or near the poles.
faq.What is a fjord?: A long, narrow, deep inlet of the sea between high cliffs, as in Norway and Iceland, typically formed by submergence of a glaciated valley.
faq.What is a plateau?: An area of relatively level high ground.
faq.What is a mesa?: An isolated flat-topped hill with steep sides, found in landscapes with horizontal strata.
faq.What is a butte?: An isolated hill with steep sides and a small flat top, found in the western United States.
faq.What is a dune?: A mound or ridge of sand or other loose sediment formed by the wind, especially on the sea coast or in a desert.
faq.What is a oasis?: A fertile spot in a desert where water is found.
faq.What is a geyser?: A hot spring in which water intermittently boils, sending a tall column of water and steam into the air.
faq.What is a volcano?: A mountain or hill, typically conical, having a crater or vent through which lava, rock fragments, hot vapor, and gas are being or have been erupted from the earth's crust.
faq.What is a earthquake?: A sudden and violent shaking of the ground, sometimes causing great destruction.
faq.What is a tsunami?: A long high sea wave caused by an earthquake, submarine landslide, or other disturbance.
faq.What is a hurricane?: A storm with a violent wind, in particular a tropical cyclone in the Caribbean.
faq.What is a tornado?: A mobile, destructive vortex of violently rotating winds having the appearance of a funnel-shaped cloud.
faq.What is a flood?: An overflow of a large amount of water beyond its normal limits, especially over what is normally dry land.
faq.What is a drought?: A prolonged period of abnormally low rainfall, leading to a shortage of water.
faq.What is a monsoon?: A seasonal prevailing wind in the region of South and Southeast Asia, blowing from the southwest between May and September and bringing rain (the wet monsoon), or from the northeast between October and April (the dry monsoon).
faq.What is a typhoon?: A tropical storm in the region of the Indian or western Pacific oceans.
faq.What is a cyclone?: A system of winds rotating inward to an area of low atmospheric pressure, with a counterclockwise (northern hemisphere) or clockwise (southern hemisphere) circulation; a depression.
faq.What is a blizzard?: A severe snowstorm with high winds and low visibility.
faq.What is a hailstorm?: A storm in which hail falls.
faq.What is a sandstorm?: A strong wind carrying clouds of sand with it, especially in a desert.
faq.What is a wildfire?: A large, destructive fire that spreads quickly over woodland or brush.
faq.What is a landslide?: The sliding down of a mass of earth or rock from a mountain or cliff.
faq.What is an avalanche?: A mass of snow, ice, and rocks falling rapidly down a mountainside.
faq.What is a meteor?: A small body of matter from outer space that enters the earth's atmosphere, becoming incandescent as a result of friction and appearing as a streak of light.
faq.What is a comet?: A celestial object consisting of a nucleus of ice and dust and, when near the sun, a tail of gas and dust particles pointing away from the sun.
faq.What is an asteroid?: A small rocky body orbiting the sun.
faq.What is a planet?: A celestial body moving in an elliptical orbit around a star.
faq.What is a star?: A luminous point in the night sky that is a large, remote incandescent body like the sun.
faq.What is a galaxy?: A system of millions or billions of stars, together with gas and dust, held together by gravitational attraction.
faq.What is a universe?: All existing matter and space considered as a whole; the cosmos.
faq.What is a black hole?: A region of space having a gravitational field so intense that no matter or radiation can escape.
faq.What is a nebula?: A cloud of gas and dust in outer space, visible in the night sky either as an indistinct bright patch or as a dark silhouette against other luminous matter.
faq.What is a supernova?: A star that suddenly increases greatly in brightness because of a catastrophic explosion that ejects most of its mass.
faq.What is a quasar?: A massive and extremely remote celestial object, emitting exceptionally large amounts of energy, and typically having a starlike image in a telescope.
faq.What is a pulsar?: A celestial object, thought to be a rapidly rotating neutron star, that emits regular pulses of radio waves and other electromagnetic radiation at rates of up to one thousand pulses per second.
faq.What is a magnetar?: A type of neutron star with an extremely powerful magnetic field.
faq.What is a wormhole?: A hypothetical connection between widely separated regions of space-time.
faq.What is dark matter?: A type of matter hypothesized to account for a large part of the total mass in the universe.
faq.What is dark energy?: A mysterious force that is driving the accelerated expansion of the universe.
faq.What is the Big Bang?: The rapid expansion of matter from a state of extremely high density and temperature that marked the origin of the universe.
faq.What is the theory of relativity?: A theory by Albert Einstein which describes the laws of physics in the presence of gravitational fields and the relationship between space and time.
faq.What is quantum mechanics?: A fundamental theory in physics describing the properties of nature on an atomic scale.
faq.What is string theory?: A theoretical framework in which the point-like particles of particle physics are replaced by one-dimensional objects called strings.
faq.What is the uncertainty principle?: A principle in quantum mechanics, formulated by Werner Heisenberg, stating that it is impossible to simultaneously determine the exact position and momentum of a particle.
faq.What is Schrödinger's cat?: A thought experiment that illustrates the concept of superposition in quantum mechanics.
faq.What is a paradox?: A statement that contradicts itself or defies intuition.
faq.What is a riddle?: A question or statement intentionally phrased so as to require ingenuity in ascertaining its answer or meaning.
faq.What is a joke?: A thing that someone says to cause amusement or laughter, especially a story with a funny punchline.
faq.What is a pun?: A joke exploiting the different possible meanings of a word or the fact that there are words which sound alike but have different meanings.
faq.What is a meme?: A humorous image, video, piece of text, etc., that is copied (often with slight variations) and spread rapidly by Internet users.
faq.What is a palindrome?: A word, phrase, or sequence that reads the same backward as forward.
faq.What is an anagram?: A word or phrase formed by rearranging the letters of another.
faq.What is a synonym?: A word or phrase that means exactly or nearly the same as another word or phrase in the same language.
faq.What is an antonym?: A word opposite in meaning to another.
faq.What is a homonym?: Each of two or more words having the same spelling or pronunciation but different meanings and origins.
faq.What is a metaphor?: A figure of speech in which a word or phrase is applied to an object or action to which it is not literally applicable.
faq.What is a simile?: A figure of speech involving the comparison of one thing with another thing of a different kind, used to make a description more emphatic or vivid.
faq.What is an idiom?: A group of words established by usage as having a meaning not deducible from those of the individual words.
faq.What is a proverb?: A short, well-known pithy saying, stating a general truth or piece of advice.
faq.What is a fable?: A short story, typically with animals as characters, conveying a moral.
faq.What is a myth?: A traditional story, especially one concerning the early history of a people or explaining a natural or social phenomenon, and typically involving supernatural beings or events.
faq.What is a legend?: A traditional story sometimes popularly regarded as historical but unauthenticated.
faq.What is a fairy tale?: A children's story about magical and imaginary beings and lands.
faq.What is a folktale?: A story originating in popular culture, typically passed on by word of mouth.
faq.What is a saga?: A long story of heroic achievement, especially a medieval prose narrative in Old Norse or Old Icelandic.
faq.What is an epic?: A long poem, typically one derived from ancient oral tradition, narrating the deeds and adventures of heroic or legendary figures or the past history of a nation.
faq.What is a sonnet?: A poem of fourteen lines using any of a number of formal rhyme schemes.
faq.What is a haiku?: A Japanese poem of seventeen syllables, in three lines of five, seven, and five.
faq.What is a limerick?: A humorous, frequently bawdy, verse of three long and two short lines rhyming aabba.
faq.What is a ballad?: A poem or song narrating a story in short stanzas.
faq.What is a narrative?: A spoken or written account of connected events; a story.
faq.What is a biography?: An account of someone's life written by someone else.
faq.What is an autobiography?: An account of a person's life written by that person.
faq.What is a memoir?: A historical account or biography written from personal knowledge.
faq.What is a diary?: A book in which one keeps a daily record of events and experiences.
faq.What is a journal?: A daily record of news and events of a personal nature; a diary.
faq.What is a logbook?: An official record; a book in which information is noted especially about a ship or aircraft's operation.
faq.What is a manuscript?: A book, document, or piece of music written by hand rather than typed or printed.
faq.What is a novel?: A fictitious prose narrative of book length, typically representing character and action with some degree of realism.
faq.What is a novella?: A short novel or long short story.
faq.What is a short story?: A story with a fully developed theme but significantly shorter and less elaborate than a novel.
faq.What is a play?: A dramatic work for the stage or to be broadcast.
faq.What is a script?: The written text of a play, film, or broadcast.
faq.What is a screenplay?: The script of a film, including acting instructions and scene directions.
faq.What is a teleplay?: A play written or adapted for television.
faq.What is a radio play?: A play for broadcasting on radio.
faq.What is a musical?: A play or movie in which singing and dancing play an essential part.
faq.What is an opera?: A dramatic work in one or more acts, set to music for singers and instrumentalists.
faq.What is a symphony?: An elaborate musical composition for full orchestra, typically in four movements.
faq.What is a concerto?: A musical composition for a solo instrument or instruments accompanied by an orchestra, especially one conceived on a relatively large scale.
faq.What is a sonata?: A composition for an instrumental soloist, often with a piano accompaniment, typically in several movements with one or more in sonata form.
faq.What is a quartet?: A group of four people playing music or singing together.
faq.What is a trio?: A group of three people playing music or singing together.
faq.What is a duet?: A performance by two people, especially singers, instrumentalists, or dancers.
faq.What is a solo?: A thing done by one person unaccompanied, in particular.
faq.What is an orchestra?: A group of instrumentalists, especially one combining string, woodwind, brass, and percussion sections and playing classical music.
faq.What is a band?: A group of musicians who play together, especially pop or rock music.
faq.What is a choir?: An organized group of singers, especially one that takes part in church services or performs regularly in public.
faq.What is a chorus?: A part of a song that is repeated after each verse.
faq.What is a verse?: Writing arranged with a metrical rhythm, typically having a rhyme.
faq.What is a refrain?: A repeated line or number of lines in a poem or song, typically at the end of each verse.
faq.What is a stanza?: A group of lines forming the basic recurring metrical unit in a poem; a verse.
faq.What is a rhyme?: Correspondence of sound between words or the endings of words.
faq.What is a rhythm?: A strong, regular, repeated pattern of movement or sound.
faq.What is a melody?: A sequence of single notes that is musically satisfying.
faq.What is harmony?: The combination of simultaneously sounded musical notes to produce chords and chord progressions having a pleasing effect.
faq.What is a chord?: A group of (typically three or more) notes sounded together, as a basis of harmony.
faq.What is a scale?: Any set of musical notes ordered by fundamental frequency or pitch.
faq.What is a key?: A group of notes based on a particular note and comprising a scale, regarded as forming the tonal basis of a piece or passage of music.
faq.What is a tempo?: The speed at which a passage of music is or should be played.
faq.What is a beat?: A main accent or rhythmic unit in music or poetry.
faq.What is a measure?: A segment of time corresponding to a specific number of beats in which each beat is represented by a particular note value.
faq.What is a rest?: An interval of silence in a piece of music.
faq.What is a clef?: A symbol placed at the beginning of the staff to indicate the pitch of the notes written on it.
faq.What is a staff?: The five lines and four spaces on which music is written.
faq.What is a note?: A symbol denoting a musical sound.
faq.What is an octave?: A series of eight notes occupying the interval between (and including) two notes, one having twice or half the frequency of vibration of the other.
faq.What is a motif?: A short musical idea, a recurring figure, musical fragment or succession of notes that has some special importance in or is characteristic of a composition.
faq.What is a theme?: The subject of a talk, a piece of writing, a person's thoughts, or an exhibition; a topic.
faq.What is a variation?: A change or slight difference in condition, amount, or level, typically within certain limits.
faq.What is a cadenza?: A virtuoso solo passage inserted into a movement in a concerto or other work, typically near the end.
faq.What is a glissando?: A continuous slide upward or downward between two notes.
faq.What is a vibrato?: A rapid, slight variation in pitch in singing or playing some musical instruments, producing a stronger or richer tone.
faq.What is a trill?: A quavering or vibratory sound, especially a rapid alternation of sung or played notes.
faq.What is a grace note?: A note printed in small type, not essential to the harmony or melody.
faq.What is a mordent?: An ornament indicating that the note is to be played with a single rapid alternation with the note above or below.
faq.What is a turn?: An ornament consisting of four notes that wind around the principal note.
faq.What is an appoggiatura?: A grace note that takes some of the value of the following note.
faq.What is an acciaccatura?: A grace note performed as quickly as possible before an essential note of a melody.
faq.What is a fermata?: A pause of unspecified length on a note or rest.
faq.What is a da capo?: A musical term meaning 'from the beginning'.
faq.What is a dal segno?: A musical term meaning 'from the sign'.
faq.What is a coda?: A concluding passage of a piece or movement, typically forming an addition to the basic structure.
faq.What is a codetta?: A short coda, especially one that concludes a section of a movement rather than the whole piece.
faq.What is a bridge?: A contrasting section that prepares for the return of the original material section.
faq.What is a prelude?: An introductory piece of music, most commonly an orchestral opening to an act of an opera, the first movement of a suite, or a short independent piece.
faq.What is an interlude?: An intervening period of time or a piece of music played between other pieces or acts.
faq.What is a postlude?: A concluding piece of music, especially an organ piece played at the end of a religious service.
faq.What is a overture?: An orchestral piece at the beginning of an opera, suite, play, oratorio, or other extended composition.
faq.What is a suite?: A set of instrumental compositions, originally in dance style, to be played in succession.
faq.What is a cantata?: A medium-length narrative piece of music for voices with instrumental accompaniment, typically with solos, chorus, and orchestra.
faq.What is an oratorio?: A large-scale musical work for orchestra and voices, typically a narrative on a religious theme, performed without the use of costumes, scenery, or action.
faq.What is a requiem?: A Mass for the repose of the souls of the dead.
faq.What is a mass?: A form of sacred musical composition, usually choral, that sets the invariable portions of the Eucharistic liturgy to music.
faq.What is a hymn?: A religious song or poem of praise to God or a god.
faq.What is a psalm?: A sacred song or hymn, in particular any of those contained in the biblical Book of Psalms and used in Christian and Jewish worship.
faq.What is a spiritual?: A religious song of a kind associated with black Christians of the southern US, and thought to derive from the combination of European hymns and African musical elements by black slaves.
faq.What is a gospel?: A fervent style of black American evangelical religious singing, developed from spirituals sung in Southern Baptist and Pentecostal churches.
faq.What is a blues?: A style of music evolved from southern African-American secular songs and usually distinguished by slow tempo and melancholy words.
faq.What is a jazz?: A type of music of black American origin characterized by improvisation, syncopation, and usually a regular or forceful rhythm.
faq.What is a rock?: A form of popular music that evolved from rock and roll and pop music during the mid- and late 1960s.
faq.What is a pop?: A genre of popular music that originated in its modern form during the mid-1950s.
faq.What is a hip hop?: A style of popular music of US black and Hispanic origin, featuring rap with an electronic backing.
faq.What is a rap?: A musical form of vocal delivery that incorporates rhyme, rhythmic speech, and street vernacular, which is performed or chanted in a variety of ways, usually over a backing beat or musical accompaniment.
faq.What is a country?: A style of popular music originating in the rural southern US.
faq.What is a folk?: Music that originates in traditional popular culture or that is written in such a style.
faq.What is a classical?: Music written in the European tradition, usually using an established form.
faq.What is a reggae?: A style of popular music with a strongly accented subsidiary beat, originating in Jamaica.
faq.What is a ska?: A style of fast popular music with a strong offbeat, originating in Jamaica in the late 1950s and an important precursor to reggae.
faq.What is a punk?: A loud, fast-moving, and aggressive form of rock music, popular in the late 1970s and early 1980s.
faq.What is a metal?: A genre of rock music characterized by a strong beat and amplified instrumental effects, typically with dark or fantastical subject matter.
faq.What is a electronic?: Music that employs electronic musical instruments and electronic music technology in its production.
faq.What is a dance?: A genre of music composed specifically to facilitate or accompany dancing.
faq.What is a trance?: A genre of electronic dance music characterized by hypnotic rhythms and sounds.
faq.What is a house?: A genre of electronic dance music characterized by a repetitive four-on-the-floor beat and a tempo of 120 to 130 beats per minute.
faq.What is a techno?: A genre of electronic dance music that emerged in Detroit, Michigan, in the United States during the mid-to-late 1980s.
faq.What is a dubstep?: A genre of electronic dance music that originated in South London in the late 1990s.
faq.What is a drum and bass?: A genre of electronic music characterized by fast breakbeats (typically between 160–180 BPM), with heavy bass and sub-bass lines.
faq.What is a ambient?: A genre of music that emphasizes tone and atmosphere over traditional musical structure or rhythm.
faq.What is a soundtrack?: Music recorded to accompany a film, video, or television program.
faq.What is a score?: The music composed for a film or play.
faq.What is a theme song?: A piece of music that is always used as the signature tune of a particular program, person, or event.
faq.What is a jingle?: A short slogan, verse, or tune designed to be easily remembered, especially as used in advertising.
faq.What is a lullaby?: A quiet, gentle song sung to send a child to sleep.
faq.What is a anthem?: A rousing or uplifting song identified with a particular group, body, or cause.
faq.What is a march?: A piece of music with a strong regular rhythm which in origin was expressly written for marching to and most frequently performed by a military band.
faq.What is a waltz?: A dance in triple time performed by a couple, who as a pair turn rhythmically around and around as they progress around the dance floor.
faq.What is a tango?: A ballroom dance originating in Buenos Aires, characterized by marked rhythms and postures and abrupt pauses.
faq.What is a salsa?: A type of Latin American dance music incorporating elements of jazz and rock.
faq.What is a samba?: A Brazilian dance of African origin.
faq.What is a bossa nova?: A style of Brazilian music derived from samba but placing more emphasis on melody and less on percussion.
faq.What is a flamenco?: A style of Spanish music, played especially on the guitar and accompanied by singing and dancing.
faq.What is a polka?: A lively dance of Bohemian origin in duple time.
faq.What is a mazurka?: A lively Polish dance in triple time.
faq.What is a bolero?: A Spanish dance in simple triple time.
faq.What is a cha-cha?: A ballroom dance with small steps and swaying hip movements, performed to a Latin American rhythm.
faq.What is a rumba?: A rhythmic dance with Spanish and African elements, originating in Cuba.
faq.What is a merengue?: A style of Dominican dance music in duple time.
faq.What is a mambo?: A Latin American dance of Cuban origin.
faq.What is a foxtrot?: A ballroom dance in quadruple time, with alternation of two slow and two quick steps.
faq.What is a quickstep?: A fast ballroom dance in 4/4 time with a fast tempo.
faq.What is a swing?: A style of jazz music with a lively rhythm suitable for dancing.
faq.What is a tap dance?: A dance performed wearing shoes fitted with metal taps, characterized by rhythmical tapping of the toes and heels.
faq.What is a ballet?: An artistic dance form performed to music using precise and highly formalized set steps and gestures.
faq.What is a modern dance?: A free, expressive style of dancing started in the early 20th century as a reaction to classical ballet.
faq.What is a contemporary dance?: A style of expressive dance that combines elements of several dance genres including modern, jazz, lyrical and classical ballet.
faq.What is a breakdance?: An athletic style of street dance from the United States, also known as breaking or b-boying.
faq.What is a street dance?: A dance style that evolved outside dance studios in any available open space such as streets, dance parties, block parties, parks, school yards, raves, and nightclubs.
faq.What is a folk dance?: A dance that reflects the life of the people of a certain country or region.
faq.What is a line dance?: A choreographed dance with a repeated sequence of steps in which a group of people dance in one or more lines or rows.
faq.What is a square dance?: A dance for four couples (eight dancers) arranged in a square, with one couple on each side, facing the middle of the square.
faq.What is a circle dance?: A dance where people stand in a circle and perform steps together.
faq.What is a contra dance?: A folk dance made up of long lines of couples.
faq.What is a ballroom dance?: A set of partner dances, which are enjoyed both socially and competitively around the world.
faq.What is a social dance?: A category of dances that have a social function and context.
faq.What is a competitive dance?: A sport in which competitors perform dances before a judge or judges for points.
faq.What is a performance dance?: A dance performed for an audience, often on a stage.
faq.What is a improvisational dance?: A dance created spontaneously, without pre-planning.
faq.What is a choreography?: The sequence of steps and movements in dance or figure skating, especially in a ballet or other staged dance.
faq.What is a dance notation?: A system for recording dance movements.
faq.What is a dance genre?: A category of dance characterized by similarities in form, style, or subject matter.
